name: Arsenic ratiometric quantifier
shortDescription: Quantifies arsenic with two reporters: an arsenic-driven green channel over a constitutive red channel. The green/red ratio cancels out cell number, growth, and instrument variation — giving a robust, self-normalizing readout for field quantitation.

input.detects: Arsenic, quantified by the green/red reporter ratio
input.operatingRange: ArsR module around the 10 µg/L WHO limit and above

sensing.sensorMechanism: Arsenite de-represses Pars; a constitutive promoter drives the normalizer reporter in parallel.
sensing.signalFlow: Arsenite -> ArsR releases Pars -> sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies the GREEN reporter (CRISPRa). A constitutive promoter drives the RED normalizer independently. The GREEN/RED ratio is the arsenic readout, self-normalized against cell density and conditions.

parts.name: ArsR + Pars
parts.source: E. coli ars operon; Stocker et al. 2003

output.readout: Green/red fluorescence ratio (plate reader / two-channel field reader)
output.result: The GREEN/RED ratio scales with arsenic, independent of cell number.

performance.limitOfDetection: ArsR module near 10 µg/L; ratiometric output improves quantitation robustness.
performance.notes: Ratiometric two-reporter normalization is a validated quantitation strategy; this arsenic CRISPRa version is a design template. The ratio cancels cell-density and instrument variation.

safety.biocontainment: Lab/contained; add kill-switch for field use.
safety.notes: E. coli K-12 non-pathogenic BSL-1 host.

buildSteps.title: Design the two channels
buildSteps.description: Signal: Pars -> CRISPRa -> sfGFP. Normalizer: J23119 -> mCherry. Design the sgRNA.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: ArsR + Pars -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> sfGFP. TU4: J23119 -> mCherry.

buildSteps.title: Transform E. coli K-12
buildSteps.description: Select; confirm stable red and arsenic-dependent green.

buildSteps.title: Arsenic titration
buildSteps.description: Run a standard curve; record green/red ratios.

buildSteps.title: Compute ratio
buildSteps.description: Interpolate arsenic from the GREEN/RED ratio.

provenance.source: Design template: a ratiometric (two-reporter) arsenic quantifier with a CRISPRa signal channel
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
